feed.title: HealthRankings — Health News
feed.subtitle: Latest US health headlines. Full list: https://healthrankings.co/healthrankings-news.html

entry.title: Mesa sees largest measles outbreak in decades, health officials urge vaccination
entry.summary: Public health officials in Maricopa County say they are confronting the most significant measles outbreak the region has seen in 30 years, with 15 confirmed cases so far in 2026.

entry.title: CDC tightens home monitoring guidance for high-risk hantavirus contacts
entry.summary: People in the “high-risk” category are instructed to stay home and limit contact with others, avoid being in buildings other than their home and avoid visitors to their residences. They are also supposed to coordinate any essential travel with their state or …

entry.title: Fourth King County resident linked to cruise ship hantavirus outbreak
entry.summary: A fourth King County resident may have been exposed to hantavirus, and someone in Chelan County is infected with a different type of hantavirus.

entry.title: Health officials track fourth King County resident tied to MV Hondius Andes hantavirus
entry.summary: Public Health – Seattle & King County officials are monitoring a fourth King County resident for possible exposure to the Andes type of hantavirus linked to the

entry.title: Sin Nombre hantavirus case tied to home mouse exposure in Chelan County
entry.summary: Chelan-Douglas Health District officials have confirmed a case of hantavirus in a Chelan County resident, marking one of the occasional infections reported eac

entry.title: Hantavirus: US has no cases of Andes hantavirus
entry.summary: A flight carrying U.S. citizens who were on the ship arrived Monday in Nebraska.

entry.title: 5 Standing Exercises That Flatten Apron Belly Faster Than Gym Workouts After 60
entry.summary: A trainer with 20+ years experience shares 5 standing moves that target apron belly after 60, no gym machines needed.

entry.title: 2 bitten by fox in Chapel Hill
entry.summary: A fox bit two people in Orange County on Friday, according to the county's animal services.

entry.title: Two Tiny Preemie Kittens Find Each Other Right When They Need It Most
entry.summary: “They may not be related by blood, but they certainly act like they are sister and brother."

entry.title: I'm A Spine Doctor. Here's 1 Thing I'd Never, Ever Do If I Had Back Pain.
entry.summary: This dangerous combination of movements is throwing out people’s backs regularly.

entry.title: People with ADHD have a natural inclination for intuitive thinking and creative breakthroughs, according to a new study
entry.summary: Researchers set out to test a long-running hunch among cognitive scientists that people with ADHD were more creative thinkers.

entry.title: CDC Didn’t Tell New York About Resident on Hantavirus-Plagued Cruise
entry.summary: There’s no indication that the New Yorker was returning to the U.S. But public health experts said the city and state still should have been informed.

entry.title: Connecticut health officials warn of hepatitis A outbreak linked to shellfish
entry.summary: The Connecticut Department of Public Health warned residents about a hepatitis A outbreak in New York that has been linked to shellfish.

entry.title: A 47-year study reveals when strength and fitness start to fade
entry.summary: A groundbreaking Swedish study that tracked people for nearly 50 years has revealed when the body’s physical decline quietly begins. Researchers found that fitness, strength, and muscle endurance start slipping around age 35, with the decline accelerating ove…

entry.title: How to get rid of mosquitoes and keep your home 100% bug free this year
entry.summary: I found the best way to get rid of mosquitoes after talking with pest control experts. They recommend the best products and methods for making your home mosquito-free.

entry.title: Scientists Say Cognitive Decline Isn’t Inevitable — Your Brain Can Improve at Any Age
entry.summary: A long-term study found that brain health can improve throughout life with consistent daily habits, cognitive training, and personalized support, regardless of age.

entry.title: Man, 53, Loses 100 Pounds and Gets Off Insulin With These 3 Lifestyle Changes
entry.summary: Jim Anders, 53, of Corpus Christi, Texas, thought feeling exhausted was part of getting older.

entry.title: Ebola outbreak with uncommon strain erupts in Congo and Uganda; 65 deaths
entry.summary: WHO learned of potential cases May 5; US CDC said it just heard about it yesterday.

entry.title: Harsh Parenting Biologically Distorts Child Stress Regulation
entry.summary: A new study uses heart monitors to prove that harsh parenting disrupts a child's biological stress co-regulation system.

entry.title: How Many Eggs Is Too Many to Eat Each Week? Nutrition Experts Explain
entry.summary: Nutrition experts explain how many eggs is too many to eat each week. They say eggs are a healthy food for most people and it's safe to eat seven to 14 eggs per week, depending on your age, medical conditions, and overall diet. Eating more could too much—they…

entry.title: Garlic Is a Secret Weapon Against Mosquitoes, Study Finds
entry.summary: Some mosquitoes and flies don't mate or lay eggs as much when exposed to a compound found in garlic.

entry.title: Scientists Studied Half A Million People And Found A Sleep Sweet Spot For Healthy Aging
entry.summary: Too little sleep was linked to faster ageing. So was too much.

entry.title: I Asked 2 Nutritionists If Sourdough Is Actually Healthier Than Other Bread, and They Both Had the Same Answer
entry.summary: The reality doesn't always match the hype.

entry.title: Cruise ships are perfect breeding ground for viruses, but we can’t get enough
entry.summary: Buffets and shared spaces help make cruise ships ideal places for viruses to flourish, disease experts say. Still, Americans are booking cruises in record numbers.

entry.title: Review of 60+ Alcohol-Caused Diseases Details Reversibility
entry.summary: A new study outlines the 60+ diseases caused by alcohol and examines which health harms are reversible.

entry.title: Suspected hantavirus case in Ontario County involves Geneva HS student, district says
entry.summary: The suspected hantavirus case in Ontario County involves a Geneva High School student, the Geneva City School District confirmed to 13WHAM.

entry.title: Is hantavirus like COVID-19? Five infectious disease experts answer some of the biggest questions about the outbreak.
entry.summary: Plus, medical experts share the virus they consider more concerning to the American public.

entry.title: Lewis Waters: Family 'heartbroken' over teen's meningitis death
entry.summary: The family of sixth form pupil Lewis Waters post a tribute following his death.

entry.title: 5 Proven Benefits of Drinking Coffee Every Day
entry.summary: Coffee is linked to several health benefits, including effects on sexual health, digestive health, metabolism, and risk of chronic disease.

entry.title: How Many Seconds Should the Abdominal Plank Last to Truly Strengthen Your Core, Glutes, and Shoulders?
entry.summary: Most people are holding the plank completely wrong. New research reveals the ideal duration is shockingly short, and pushing past it does more harm than good.

entry.title: Scientists Solve a 60-Year-Old Fat Cell Mystery — and It Changes What We Know About Obesity
entry.summary: A decades-old assumption about how the body handles fat may have been incomplete.

entry.title: The brain’s “feel good” chemical may be secretly fueling tinnitus
entry.summary: Scientists have uncovered evidence that serotonin — the same brain chemical boosted by many antidepressants — may actually worsen tinnitus. Using advanced light-based brain stimulation in mice, researchers identified a serotonin-driven circuit linked directly…

entry.title: A 4-Week Diet Change May Turn Back The Clock on Aging, Study Suggests
entry.summary: We all get old eventually if we're lucky, but not always at the same rate.

entry.title: Routine vaccines may cut dementia risk—experts have startling hypothesis on how
entry.summary: Vaccines may be training a part of our immune system long thought to be untrainable.

entry.title: The promises and pitfalls of personalized health
entry.summary: While health tech companies are chasing personalized health, the reality is algorithms and features don’t really take chronic conditions into account.

entry.title: This biomarker is a better predictor of heart disease than cholesterol: What to know about C-reactive protein.
entry.summary: The Conversation reports C-reactive protein is a superior heart disease risk marker over cholesterol, urging universal screening for better prevention.

entry.title: A Newly Discovered Virus Weak Spot Could Change Future Treatments
entry.summary: Scientists have identified a hidden weakness shared by several viruses, a discovery that could influence the future antiviral treatments.

entry.title: 15,000 Zombie Deer Are Eaten by Humans Each Year, and That Number is Growing
entry.summary: Learn how chronic wasting disease spreads, why infected “zombie deer” act strangely, and whether humans are at risk.

entry.title: Why Do We Sneeze? An Evolutionary Biologist Explains
entry.summary: Why does a sneeze take over your whole face? And why can light trigger it in some people? The answers lie in brain evolution and sensory wiring.

entry.title: Thailand mandates 42-day hantavirus quarantine
entry.summary: Thailand has classified hantavirus as a dangerous communicable disease despite no cases having been detected in the country, saying that any high-risk contacts of infected persons must quarantine for 42 days.

entry.title: Visiting Museums May Slow Your Biological Aging, Study Finds
entry.summary: Several years ago, some doctors in Canada began 'prescribing' art to their patients, giving them free access to a local museum.

entry.title: What Happens to Your Liver When You Drink Coffee Every Day
entry.summary: Discover how coffee can support liver health by reducing the risk of fibrosis and cirrhosis, lowering enzymes and providing antioxidant protection.

entry.title: This Is the Best Type of Nutrient to Combat Alzheimer’s Disease, According to Dietitians
entry.summary: A growing body of research suggests that regularly eating this affordable protein may lower Alzheimer's disease risk.

entry.title: Bryan Johnson says you are not drinking coffee for the caffeine — you are drinking it for the gut microbes that run your brain, and the research behind that claim is harder to dismiss than it sounds
entry.summary: My mornings run on a specific sequence. Kettle on. Grinder out. The smell before the first pull. The way the crema settles if you did everything right. I am not someone who merely drinks coffee. I am someone who makes it with the kind of attention I give very…

entry.title: HaysMed, doctor sued for $10 million after death of patient during heart surgery
entry.summary: File photo By CRISTINA JANNEYHays PostHaysMed and one of its doctors h

entry.title: Penile implant specialist with history of far-right comments led Hantavirus presser
entry.summary: As the Trump administration sought to reassure Americans this week that a potential hantavirus outbreak posed little risk to the public, Dr. Brian Christine, one of the top public health officials in charge of infectious disease policy, stood before reporters…

entry.title: Emergency scientific consultation on Andes Virus medical countermeasures (MCM) R&D
entry.summary: The consultation will include discussions on:current epidemiological developments and transmission dynamicshistorical and recent evidence on human-to-human transmissionclinical management and natural history studiesdiagnostics, therapeutics, and vaccine MCM l…

entry.title: Ebola outbreak kills 65 in eastern DR Congo's Ituri province
entry.summary: Africa's top health agency says around 246 cases have been reported.

entry.title: Africa top health body confirms new Ebola outbreak in remote Congo province
entry.summary: Africa's top public health body has confirmed a new Ebola outbreak in Congo’s remote Ituri province. The Africa CDC reported 246 suspected cases and 65 deaths so far. Most cases and deaths are in the Mongwalu and Rwampara health zones. The agency said that fo…

entry.title: Hantavirus case suspected in Ontario County
entry.summary: Ontario County is investigating a suspected case of locally acquired hantavirus, according to a release from Kate Ott, the county's public health director.
